pages.raw_text:
The petition of the Washington
Water Power company for writ pro-
hibiting the city from interfering
with the company while laying it's
track on Third avenue was denied by
Judge KEENAN, yesterday


The court held that it was without
power to grant the writ at any time
before the trial of the action brought
to ascertain the rights of the parties.
The action of Judge KEENAN, will in
no way affect the merits of the case.
1t was merely a question as to
whether the court had the power to
grant the writ that was decided.


The company holds that under
franchise granted in 1890 it has the
right to construct the track. 1t de-
MES the right of the city to exclusive
control of all the streets, but admits
that the city can control the manner
in which the tracks are constructed.
